text_draft:
Pink Bear looked at the snow. Then at the toy spoon on Susie's floor.
She could accept the indoor hunt. It was safe. It was warm.
Or she could try.
Pink Bear picked up the spoon in her stubby paws and climbed onto the windowsill. She pressed her nose against the cold glass and began to scratch. Scrape. Scrape.
The spoon was too small. Her arms were too short. The snow didn't move.
But Pink Bear kept trying. Scrape. Scrape. Her button eyes never left the garden.
Ruby watched from her corner, ancient and still.
Pink Bear didn't stop.
